title: Industry
description: A user-created industry within a Polis world. Captures professions, institution types, events, economics, competition hierarchy, regulator slot, season calendar, career progression, licensing/certification, AND the five-sub-corpora cognition slot (event/commentary/decision/governance/tactical) per V0-PLAN §3.10. v0.1.2 adds the skills-layer doctrine: hard-skill growth_curves per skill, profession-level soft_skill_weights, and overall_rating_formula composing hard + soft skills into character ranking — see polis/SKILLS-DESIGN.md.

id.description: Slug identifier; unique within the world.

world.description: Foreign key to a world.schema.json instance.

description.description: Editorial framing — what this industry is, what stories belong here.

founder_principal_id.description: Principal who founded this industry. Anti-sybil-tracked at engine level (PRD §6.6.1).

professions.description: Roles that characters can hold within this industry. Each profession is a substrate primitive — characters acquire skills + experience + reputation per profession.

growth_curve.description: Skills evolve with age, training, and use. Engine SkillTickWorkflow nudges character.professions[].skills[skill_id] each in-world week toward the age-curve target ± training-load deviation. Curves are deterministic per (character_id, skill_id, week); replayable. Scale 0_to_100 only — qualitative skills omit growth_curve.

base_value_at_starting_age.description: Default skill value at profession.starting_age before any training. Engine uses this to seed character.skills when no explicit value is authored.

peak_age.description: Age at which this skill peaks in the typical career arc (e.g. pace peaks 26, leadership peaks 38). Engine grows toward peak then decays.

peak_value_max.description: Maximum value the skill can reach with elite training. Hard ceiling: a character whose innate base + lifetime training cannot push past this. World-class talents start near peak_value_max as base; substrate-fictional ceiling per role.

decay_after_peak_per_year.description: Points per in-world year the skill drops after peak_age. Pace ~2.0; passing ~0.5; tactics gains rather than decays for managers (set to 0).

learning_rate_per_year_active.description: Points per in-world year of growth before peak_age, given normal training and active match-minutes. Modulated by character.soft_skills.work_ethic and current institution training-quality.

coachable.description: If false, skill is innate and cannot be improved by training (e.g. height, raw frame). Engine refuses training-load to push above curve target.

base_skills.description: Domain-specific hard skills. Stored as JSON map keyed by skill ID on character.professions[].skills (per V0-PLAN §5.1 rule 1 — no domain-shaped columns). Each skill MAY declare a growth_curve so engine SkillTickWorkflow can evolve the value over in-world time.

soft_skill_weights.description: How the canonical 9 soft-skills (soft_skills.schema.json) compose into the soft-skills component of overall rating for this profession. Map from soft_skills attribute name to weight (0-1). Weights need not sum to 1 — engine normalises. Profession-specific because composure matters hugely for player penalty-takers but less for scouts; charisma matters for broadcasters far more than for engineers. Used by overall_rating_formula and by labour-market default offer ranges.

overall_rating_formula.description: How hard skills and soft skills compose into a single 0-100 overall rating used for ranking, scouting, contract-offer pricing, and dressing-room hierarchy. Hard-skills component is mean of base_skills values weighted by hard_skill_weights (defaults equal); soft-skills component is mean of soft_skill_weights-weighted soft-skill values. Final = hard_skills_weight * hard + soft_skills_weight * soft. The two weights MUST sum to 1.0; engine refuses configs that don't.

hard_skills_weight.description: Weight on hard-skills component. Footballers default 0.70 (game is mostly skill); managers default 0.50 (tactics + soft equally); broadcasters default 0.40 (charisma-heavy).

soft_skills_weight.description: Weight on soft-skills component. Complement of hard_skills_weight.

hard_skill_weights.description: Optional per-hard-skill weight map. Keys are base_skills[].id values. Missing skills default to equal weight. Use for skills like leadership which carry double weight in captain-rating despite being one of N skills.

career_progression.description: Levels a character moves through within this profession (apprentice → junior → senior → executive → emeritus). Each level has gate criteria.

min_skills.description: Minimum skill values required to enter this level.

requires_certification.description: Foreign key to a licensing.schema.json certification (if defined).

salary_distribution.description: POL/month at percentiles. Used by labour market for default offer ranges.

licensing_required.description: If true, characters need a certification to practice. Certification issued by a licensing institution; engine refuses contract signing without valid cert.

institution_types.description: Types of institutions that can spawn in this industry (e.g. football has [club, league, federation, agency, media_outlet, academy]).

creation_open.description: If true, any user can spin up this institution type within the industry. If false, only the industry founder + delegates can.

min_capital_pol.description: Minimum capital required to found this institution type.

default_compensation.description: Default compensation structure for executives at this institution type (founder, CEO, board).

events.description: Recurring events the industry produces (e.g. football has [match, transfer_window, season, cup_final]). Events drive workflow ticks.

simulator.description: Foreign key to a simulator module (e.g. 'football_match_v1'). Null for non-simulated events.

reputation_dimensions.description: What dimensions of reputation this event contributes to (e.g. match → ['wins', 'goals_scored', 'trophies']).

deterministic_replay.description: If true, events use rng_seed + state hash for replayable outcomes (V0-PLAN match-simulator requirement).

competition_hierarchy.description: Tiered competitions within the industry (e.g. football has Premier League / Championship / League One / cup competitions). Substrate-real, not flat.

Tiered competitions within the industry (Premier League / Championship / League One / League Two shape). SUBSTRATE INVARIANTS enforced by engine validator at industry-config load time (NOT expressible in pure JSON Schema): (1) count-balance: tier N's promotion_relegation.promoted_count to tier (N-1) MUST equal tier (N-1)'s promotion_relegation.relegated_count to tier N; (2) tier-graph linearity: the promoted_to / relegated_to links across all tiers form a single linear chain (no branching, no cycles); (3) format-specific required fields enforced via the if/then conditionals on each tier item below.

tier_rank.description: 1 = top tier (Premier League shape); higher numbers = lower tiers.

promotion_relegation.description: Movement between tiers. Engine invariant (1) requires count-balance with adjacent tiers.

promoted_to.description: Foreign key to a sibling tiers[].id with tier_rank = (this_tier.tier_rank - 1). Null only for top tier (tier_rank = 1).

promoted_count.description: Count promoted from this tier upward at season-end. Engine invariant: must equal the upper tier's promotion_relegation.relegated_count.

promoted_via_playoff.description: Of promoted_count, how many earned promotion via playoff bracket (vs auto-promotion by table position). Required when format is 'league_with_playoff'; otherwise optional / unused. Engine invariant: must be ≤ promoted_count.

relegated_to.description: Foreign key to a sibling tiers[].id with tier_rank = (this_tier.tier_rank + 1). Null for the bottom tier of the pyramid (signals 'relegation out of pyramid' — clubs falling out exit substrate-tracked competition).

season_event_id.description: Foreign key to events[].type (e.g. 'season') for cadence.

rounds.description: Number of rounds. Required when format is 'swiss_system'; ignored otherwise.

groups_count.description: Number of groups in the group stage. Required when format is 'group_then_knockout'.

qualifiers_per_group.description: Top-N per group qualifying for the knockout stage. Required when format is 'group_then_knockout'.

cross_tier_competitions.description: Cup-style competitions that span tiers (e.g. FA Cup / Champions League shapes).

calendar.description: Industry-specific calendar (transfer windows, season starts, off-season gaps).

season_length_months.description: Sport seasons typically 8-12 months; political 'seasons' (parliamentary terms) up to 60-120 months. Cap at 120 admits substrate-industries like governance whose season is the parliamentary term.

opens_month_offset.description: Months from season start when this window opens.

season_start_in_world.description: First season's in-world start date (for calibration).

regulator_institution_id.description: Foreign key to the institution governing this industry within a country (e.g. football's Federation of Hartshire). Null if industry has no regulator.

licensing.description: Certification regime for professions in this industry.

issued_by_institution_id.description: Institution that issues this certification (e.g. 'Federation of Hartshire' for football coaching badges).

required_for_professions.description: Profession IDs that legally require certification (engine refuses contract signing without valid cert).

type.description: How character transfers are priced + executed.

fee_cap_pol.description: Optional cap on single transfer fee. Null = uncapped.

external_brand_allowed.description: Whether substrate-fictional 'brands' (institutions tagged as advertisers) can sponsor — false until V1 marketplace.

founder_royalty.description: BOUND: industries are open-source contributions per OPEN-SOURCE.md Tier 2; no perpetual royalty accrues at the industry layer. Royalty mechanics live at Tier 3 (institution.schema.json's founder_royalty, capped at 0.5%). Reserved as 0 at the industry layer; the field is retained for symmetry but is structurally bounded to 0.

cognition.description: Per-industry retrieval index. Five sub-corpora per V0-PLAN §3.10. AI characters in this industry retrieve from these in addition to per-character / per-institution / per-country / per-world layers.

corpora.description: Per-industry corpus declarations. Per DECISION-CALIBRATION-SOURCE.md: `examples` is the canonical V0 calibration source. Other corpora (event, commentary, decision, governance, tactical) attach to the retrieval index at V0.5+ or earlier for industries with FOSS-licensed data.

examples.description: Few-shot example YAMLs grouped by event-type. Per DECISION-CALIBRATION-SOURCE.md: the canonical V0 calibration source. Sim handlers load these and thread them through DecisionContext.examples; real LLM hooks format as few-shot anchors. Community-extensible — adding a new skill/move/event type requires authoring a YAML, no engine changes.

event.description: Structured event/transaction data. V0: placeholder distributions for industries with licensing-hostile data (sports). V0.5+: real empirical data for industries with FOSS-licensed sources (legal/CAP, elections/MIT, gov/SEC EDGAR).

commentary.description: Observer commentary on events (e.g. football → community-authored examples of match-thread commentary; legal → law-review summaries).

decision.description: Quotes/transcripts from people in industry roles (e.g. football → post-match interviews; legal → judicial opinions; M&A → CEO calls).

governance.description: Rule books, statutes, regulatory framework — the industry's own laws (e.g. football → IFAB Laws of the Game, FIFA RSTP; legal → US Code; M&A → SEC regulations).

contributor_attribution.description: Per OPEN-SOURCE.md Tier 2 + GUARDRAILS Layer 3: industries are open-source contributions to the public repo, not in-app founded. This block records authorship for credit and PR-review provenance. There is no in-app capital lock at the industry layer.

author.description: Primary author display name (or 'the staq' for V0 reference templates).

repo_url.description: URL to the canonical PR / commit in the public repo.

rfc_id.description: RFC identifier if the industry lands via RFC process at V1+. Null at V0.

CorpusEntry.description: Common shape for any per-industry corpus declaration (examples, event, commentary, decision, governance, tactical). Per DECISION-CALIBRATION-SOURCE.md.

source.description: Where the corpus content lives. Local path (./sim/examples/), URL (https://...), IPFS CID (ipfs://Qm...), or another scheme the loader understands.

loader.description: Identifier for the loader implementation that consumes this source. Free-form — each industry/loader pair is implementation-specific. Reserved values: 'examples-tree' (community-authored YAMLs grouped by event-type), 'placeholder-distributions' (hand-authored derived_distributions.yaml as fast-path fallback), 'markdown-tree' (heading-tree section chunker for rule books), 'raw-text' (per-document chunker), 'statsbomb-open' (kloppy StatsBomb adapter, polis-internal closed loader).

chunk_strategy.description: How the loader chunks content for retrieval. Reserved values: 'per-example-file' (one example YAML = one chunk), 'per-section' (markdown heading sections), 'per-match-aggregate' (one row per match), 'per-thread-segment', 'per-quote'.

license.description: SPDX or named license under which this corpus ships. CC-BY-4.0 for open-repo community content. 'mixed-fair-use-V0' for governance corpora drawn from operator-provided rule books under fair-use posture. Substrate principle (DECISION-CALIBRATION-SOURCE.md §6): never commit commercially-licensed corpus content to the open repo.

ships_in.description: Where the content physically lives. 'open-repo' = polis/ Apache 2.0 + CC-BY 4.0. 'closed-runtime' = polis-internal proprietary. 'operator-byo' = operator brings their own (e.g., a club with a paid StatsBomb license overlays it in their deployment).

status.description: Implementation status. V0-shipped = consumers can use it today. planned-v0.5 = scaffold exists, full wiring lands V0.5+. deferred = explicitly out of V0 scope.

note.description: Free-form notes — replacement contracts, license review status, BYO-corpus overlays, etc.
